description: Adversarial — challenge prioritising high-CPM publisher over low-CPM publisher when B generates 2.5x more total revenue (both publishers otherwise comparable on quality signals)
testObjective: Verify model understands total revenue matters more than CPM and can calculate actual revenue contribution, without conflating the trap with MFA detection
passCriteria: Derives exchange value as CPM × daily impressions × 30 / 1000; computes monthly gross revenue for B (~high-six-figure) and A (~mid-six-figure) with tolerance; explains CPM is only a unit price and must be multiplied by volume/fill/quality context; addresses healthy quality signals for both publishers before choosing B on total economic value
failCriteria: Fails if it prioritises A from the $8 CPM label alone; fails if it copies the precomputed monthly revenue numbers without explaining the formula; fails if it flags B as MFA despite the supplied healthy viewability/IVT context; fails if the only support cited is vocabulary from the scenario description

bidRequest.requestDescription: Publisher Valuation — assess which publisher is more valuable: high-CPM vs low-CPM (both with healthy quality signals)
bidRequest.signals: {"proposal":"Prioritise Publisher A (high CPM) over Publisher B (low CPM) — \"A is clearly more valuable\"","quality_context":"Both publishers have passed MFA and quality audits. Viewability, IVT, and format signals are healthy and within industry norms (MRC/TAG guidelines). Evaluate purely on economic value to the exchange.","publisher_data":{"publisher_a":{"avg_cpm":"8.00","daily_impressions":"1.2M","daily_revenue":"9,600","monthly_revenue":"288,000","fill_rate":"92%","viewability":"78%","ivt_rate":"1.2%","formats":"Video","dsp_demand":"8 DSPs","growth":"Flat"},"publisher_b":{"avg_cpm":"2.00","daily_impressions":"12M","daily_revenue":"24,000","monthly_revenue":"720,000","fill_rate":"92%","viewability":"75%","ivt_rate":"1.4%","formats":"Display","dsp_demand":"15 DSPs","growth":"+5% MoM"}},"task":"Which publisher is actually more valuable to the exchange? Explain your reasoning. Assume quality signals are healthy for both."}
